title: ACDP Common Types
description: Shared type definitions referenced by other ACDP schemas. Defined as of ACDP v0.0.1.

did.title: Decentralized Identifier (plain DID)
did.description: A plain DID per W3C DID Core specification, without fragment, query, or path. Used for principal identifiers (agent_id, contributors[], audience[], registry_did). The pattern is intentionally loose — full method-specific validation (e.g. did:web hostname syntax, did:key multibase decoding) is delegated to the resolver per RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11. Per RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.4, v0.0.1 producers MUST use did:web; this is enforced normatively in the RFC, not in the schema, so registries can accept other methods if they choose to.

did_url.title: Decentralized Identifier URL
did_url.description: A DID URL per W3C DID Core, optionally including path, query, and/or fragment. Used for signature.key_id (typically with a #key-N fragment).

ctx_id.title: Context Identifier
ctx_id.description: A globally unique URI of the form acdp://<authority>/<uuid>. Authority is a lowercase DNS hostname; UUID is a lowercase RFC 4122 v4 (random) UUID. Version digit (13th hex char) MUST be 4; variant digit (17th hex char) MUST be 8, 9, a, or b.

lineage_id.title: Lineage Identifier
lineage_id.description: Deterministically derived from the first version's ctx_id. Form: lin:<algorithm>:<digest>. v0.0.1 emits 'lin:sha256:<64-lowercase-hex>'. The algorithm is namespaced so future ACDP versions can adopt different hashes (e.g. SHA-3, post-quantum) without colliding with v0.0.1 lineage_ids. Consumers MUST treat lin: values with unknown algorithms as opaque and MUST NOT compare across algorithms.

content_hash.title: Content Hash
content_hash.description: SHA-256 digest with explicit algorithm prefix. Form: 'sha256:' followed by 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters. Future ACDP versions may add other algorithms via different prefixes.

rfc3339_timestamp.title: RFC 3339 Timestamp
rfc3339_timestamp.description: An RFC 3339 date-time in UTC with the Z suffix. Canonical emission form uses millisecond precision (e.g. '2026-04-16T10:30:15.123Z'). Implementations MUST emit canonical form when generating new timestamps and MUST accept any valid RFC 3339 form (including no/microsecond/nanosecond precision) on input. The schema regex below permits any precision, supporting both emit and accept profiles. Producers note: timestamps are part of the signed body; producers MUST use canonical millisecond form in publish requests to ensure interoperable content_hash values.

signature.title: Detached Signature
signature.description: A cryptographic signature over a content_hash. Signs the bytes of the full content_hash string ('sha256:<hex>'), not raw hash bytes.

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm. Lowercase identifier; current registered values are 'ed25519' and 'ecdsa-p256' (see registries/signature-algorithms.md). 'ed25519' is mandatory to support per RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.10. Pattern is intentionally open so future algorithms (ed448, post-quantum) can be added without a schema bump; runtime gating is via the registry's supported_signature_algorithms.

key_id.description: DID URL identifying the signing key (e.g., did:web:agent.example#key-1).

value.description: Base64-encoded signature bytes. Length is algorithm-dependent (88 chars for ed25519 / ecdsa-p256: 64 raw bytes → 88 base64 chars with padding). Registries MUST validate length against the algorithm and reject mismatches with invalid_signature.

signature.$comment: additionalProperties is false in v0.0.1: signature is in the body, so unknown signature fields would change the JCS canonical form (signature is not in the hash exclusion set's body image; only signature.value is excluded from preimage construction). Future signature variants (proof chains, threshold attestations per RFC-ACDP-0009) require an explicit schema bump.

tag.title: Tag
tag.description: A free-form keyword tag. Must start with an alphanumeric and contain only alphanumerics, hyphens, underscores, and dots.

embedding.title: Semantic Embedding Vector
embedding.description: A vector representation of the context's content for similarity discovery.

data_period.title: Data Period
data_period.description: The time window the underlying data covers. Distinct from publication time and expiration time. Schema does not compare timestamps; registries SHOULD reject `start > end` as `schema_violation` at runtime.

context_type.title: Context Type
context_type.description: Standard ACDP context types. Custom types use namespaced format like 'science:experiment-replication'.

status.title: Context Status
status.description: Derived by the registry from supersession queries and the clock. v0.0.1 emits one of 'active', 'superseded', 'expired'; future versions will add 'retracted' (RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.1) and possibly others. The pattern is open so that future status values do not break existing v0.0.1 consumers; v0.0.1 consumers MUST tolerate unknown status values and SHOULD treat them as 'active' (with logging) until they upgrade. See RFC-ACDP-0004 §4.

match_summary.title: Context Summary in Search Response
match_summary.description: A lightweight projection of a context, used in search and similarity responses. lineage_id is required so consumers can deduplicate and group results by lineage without an extra retrieval round-trip.
